title: Highest temperature in London on July 15?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the London City Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Met Office guidance and ensemble forecasts indicate a weakening high-pressure system over northwest Europe will likely deliver peak temperatures of 28–29°C in London on July 15, with limited further warming expected amid increasing cloud and possible scattered showers. These conditions follow an early-July heatwave that produced 35°C readings but has since moderated under shifting Atlantic flow. Model consensus shows modest uncertainty in boundary-layer mixing and wind direction, keeping 28°C and 29°C closely matched in trader-implied probabilities while assigning lower weight to 30°C or higher thresholds. Updated short-range runs and Met Office briefings through July 14 will refine the final maximum before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris faces an intense July 2026 heatwave driven by persistent high pressure and southerly advection of warm air masses, pushing official Météo-France guidance toward afternoon maxima in the low-to-mid 30s Celsius for July 15. Ensemble model spreads from ECMWF and others create genuine uncertainty around exact peak values, with small differences in cloud cover, boundary-layer mixing, or timing of any weak frontal passage able to shift the daily high by 1–2°C. This forecast variability, set against historical July extremes rarely exceeding 35°C in central Paris, supports the tightly bunched market-implied odds across the 30–34°C brackets as traders weigh the latest high-resolution runs and urban heat-island amplification.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent heat wave advisories across South Korea, combined with East Asian monsoon moisture and variable cloud cover, anchor trader sentiment around 28–30°C for Seoul’s July 15 high. Official Korea Meteorological Administration monitoring shows persistent high humidity and scattered afternoon convection that can suppress or enhance daytime maxima by 1–2°C depending on timing. Subtle differences in steering flow and urban heat-island effects differentiate the closely matched 27–30°C outcomes, while ensemble model spread remains modest this close to the event. Historical July climatology places typical highs near 29°C, supporting the tight clustering of market-implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecasts from the Turkish Meteorological Service and international models converge on a high of 29–30°C for Ankara on July 15, 2026, under stable high-pressure conditions typical of mid-summer continental climates. This drives the market’s heavy weighting toward 29°C, as recent runs show light winds, minimal cloud cover, and no significant frontal systems or heat advection expected in the next 48 hours. Historical July averages near 31–32°C provide context, but the immediate pattern reflects a slight moderation from early-month warmth. Traders appear to price in limited upside risk from model spread or urban heat-island effects, with resolution hinging on official maximum readings at the central station.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent Southwest Monsoon conditions over Singapore favor daily maximum temperatures clustered near 32–33°C for July 15, with afternoon thundery showers and variable cloud cover acting as the main short-term modulators. Official NEA outlooks note maxima typically between 33–34°C on clearer days but lower when localized showers develop by late morning, reducing insolation and enhancing evaporative cooling. Recent drier spells in the second week of July have slightly elevated the chance of reaching 33°C by limiting persistent cloud decks, yet model consensus still shows high humidity and southeasterly flow keeping extremes rare. Traders price 32°C and 33°C nearly equally because small shifts in shower timing or convective intensity—resolved only in the final 24–48 hours—can swing the recorded high by one degree, consistent with Singapore’s narrow historical July range around 31–32°C.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chongqing Jiangbei International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Chongqing Jiangbei International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/chongqing/ZUCK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see roughly even odds on a 39–41°C peak for Chongqing on July 15 because short-range models diverge on how long the current heatwave will sustain extreme highs. The city recorded its first 40°C day of 2026 on July 10 amid a broad subtropical ridge, with official readings reaching 41°C shortly afterward; persistence of this pattern through mid-month under weak steering flow and high surface humidity favors another 40–41°C day. Countervailing signals include some ensemble runs showing modest cooling from increased cloud cover or a weak frontal passage around the 14th–15th, which could cap the daily maximum near 37–39°C. Chongqing’s urban heat-island effect and topographic sheltering amplify daytime maxima relative to regional averages, yet day-to-day variability remains high this close to the target date, producing the tight clustering of implied probabilities around the 39–41°C outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Beijing's short-term numerical weather forecasts show a spread across 34–37°C for the July 15 daily maximum, reflecting model disagreement on the strength and position of the western Pacific subtropical high and monsoon moisture transport. Clear, subsiding air under a stronger ridge favors 36–37°C readings, while increased cloud cover, scattered thunderstorms, or a slight southward rain-belt shift could cap the peak near 34–35°C. The urban heat-island effect and seasonal July climatology (historical midsummer highs clustering 31–35°C) underpin the market’s concentration around these brackets, with lower probabilities for extremes reflecting limited potential for either strong cold advection or record heat in the current pattern. Updated CMA and global model runs over the next 48 hours remain the key near-term catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts for Wuhan indicate a daily high near 35–36°C on July 15, shaped by the lingering effects of heavy rainfall and moisture from Super Typhoon Bavi, which prompted warnings across Hubei province through July 13. Cloud cover and precipitation associated with the storm system and associated troughs are expected to moderate daytime heating compared with peak-summer climatology, when afternoon maxima often reach 34–38°C. Ensemble guidance shows modest spread around these values, with limited potential for brief clearing that could push readings toward 37°C. Traders appear to weigh the balance between this near-term cooling influence and Wuhan’s typical mid-July tendency for rapid warming once rain ends, resulting in tightly matched probabilities for the 36°C and 37°C outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ensemble guidance from ECMWF and GFS models points to daytime maxima of 36–38°C in Chengdu on July 15 amid the Sichuan Basin’s subtropical monsoon regime, where high humidity (70–80%) and afternoon convective clouds often cap extreme heating. Historical July climatology shows frequent 35–38°C readings tempered by monsoon moisture, while any shift toward clearer skies or reduced precipitation would favor higher bins. Updated model runs and official China Meteorological Administration briefings over the next 48 hours remain the key variables traders are monitoring.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 28–31°C for Shenzhen’s July 15 maximum because short-range forecast models show persistent subtropical moisture and scattered thunderstorm activity that will modulate peak heating.** High humidity near 80 % and variable cloud cover from monsoon-driven convection are expected to limit insolation, keeping daytime maxima close to the July climatological average of 30–32 °C while preventing extreme values above 33 °C. Recent model runs highlight sensitivity to the exact timing and coverage of showers: clearer breaks could push readings to 31 °C, whereas earlier or heavier rain would cap them nearer 29 °C. With resolution only 48 hours away, traders are weighting the latest CMA and global ensemble guidance on boundary-layer moisture and steering flow, which continue to produce a narrow distribution centered on these four outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble model consensus from ECMWF and GFS runs currently favors a July 15 daily maximum near 27–28°C in Istanbul, consistent with mid-month climatology of 27–29°C. Short-range forecast uncertainty remains elevated two days out, with traders tracking the next 12Z and 00Z model cycles plus Turkish State Meteorological Service updates for refinements ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecasts from agencies including the Met Office and BBC place Amsterdam's July 15 high near 28–30 °C under a building high-pressure ridge, supporting the market's leading 28 °C outcome at 33 % implied probability. Model consensus shows modest warming from early-July coastal cooling, yet spread remains wide because small shifts in Atlantic trough position, low-level cloud, or easterly flow can trim or boost daytime maxima by 2–3 °C. Historical July averages near 22 °C underscore the above-normal signal, while resolution hinges on official KNMI observations rather than model output. Traders appear to price in this narrow but uncertain window ahead of final model runs and the 48-hour forecast update.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent official forecasts from the Finnish Meteorological Institute and European models project a daily maximum around 24–25°C for Helsinki on July 15, 2026, under mostly cloudy skies with light northeasterly flow and limited solar heating. eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models for Jeddah on July 15 show daily highs clustered near 36–38 °C, producing the tight market split among the 36 °C, 37 °C, and 38 °C+ buckets. Persistent subtropical high pressure supports strong solar heating, while Red Sea sea-surface temperatures above the 1991–2020 July baseline sustain elevated humidity that modulates peak readings through latent-heat effects and reduced nocturnal cooling. Light to moderate onshore flow and sea-breeze timing introduce day-to-day variability of 1–2 °C, and ensemble spread in global and regional models reflects lingering uncertainty over exact ridge strength and boundary-layer moisture. Recent mid-July observations around 35–37 °C reinforce the narrow range, with new model runs and official updates from Saudi meteorological sources expected to refine the outcome before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters around 31–33°C for Guangzhou’s July 15 maximum, reflecting short-range model guidance from the China Meteorological Administration and international ensembles that show afternoon temperatures near the seasonal 33°C average but tempered by scattered thunderstorms and cloud cover expected to limit peak heating. Subtropical monsoon conditions, with high humidity and southerly flow, favor convective development that can suppress daytime highs by 1–2°C on any given day, while clearer intervals allow brief spikes toward 34°C. Recent broader heat anomalies across southern China have kept probabilities for sub-30°C outcomes low, yet the tight spread among leading bins underscores forecast uncertainty in exact convective timing and intensity over the next 48 hours.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ensemble forecasts for Qingdao on July 15 indicate modest uncertainty around peak temperatures near 29–31 °C, aligning with the market’s tight clustering of implied probabilities on those outcomes. Persistent easterly onshore flow and scattered convective activity over the past several days have suppressed insolation and held observed highs near 26–28 °C, consistent with climatological July averages of 27–28 °C. Model spread arises primarily from differences in the timing and coverage of afternoon thunderstorms versus any clearing that could allow stronger surface heating, with stronger ridging or delayed precipitation potentially lifting readings into the low 30s. Updated short-range guidance and satellite trends over the next 48 hours will be the key drivers refining resolution odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see 34°C as the most likely high for Lucknow on July 15 because active monsoon conditions are expected to deliver cloud cover, scattered rainfall, and elevated humidity that typically cap afternoon temperatures near or slightly above the July average of 33–34°C. Recent IMD observations show maximums fluctuating between 33°C and 36°C amid variable thundershowers, with model guidance indicating possible breaks in rain that could allow brief warming to 35–36°C or keep readings closer to 33°C depending on steering winds and moisture influx. This narrow range of outcomes reflects genuine uncertainty in the timing and intensity of monsoon surges over northern India, where even small shifts in cloudiness or wind patterns can alter peak readings by 1–2°C. Updated short-range forecasts from the India Meteorological Department over the next 48 hours will likely refine these probabilities ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts for Cape Town point to a typical mid-July high near the 16–18°C range, aligning with the market's tight clustering around these outcomes and the region's Mediterranean winter climate moderated by the cold Benguela Current. Historical July averages near 17°C, with daily maxima rarely exceeding 21°C, provide the baseline, while recent cool conditions including morning fog and weak cold fronts have kept highs near or below normal. Key differentiating factors include Atlantic high-pressure positioning that can suppress or enhance warming, variable cloud cover affecting solar heating, and light winds that limit mixing; stronger offshore flow or clearer skies could push toward 18–19°C, whereas persistent marine influence favors 16°C or lower. Updated ECMWF and SAWS model runs over the next 48 hours will refine resolution criteria based on official maximum temperature observations.

title: Highest temperature in Karachi on July 15?

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts from models and agencies like the Pakistan Meteorological Department point to peak temperatures of 32–33°C in Karachi on July 15 under typical early-monsoon conditions, with above-normal seasonal temperatures offset by coastal moderation. Persistent southwesterly flow brings moderate humidity and scattered cloud cover that limits daytime heating, while the Arabian Sea breeze often intensifies in the afternoon to cap maxima near the lower end of the range. Any increase in low-level moisture or brief convective activity could further suppress readings to 32°C, whereas clearer intervals would allow brief spikes toward 34°C. Historical July averages hover around 33°C, but day-to-day variability from these factors keeps the two leading outcomes nearly tied in trader assessments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus around 21–23°C for São Paulo’s July 15 maximum reflects the city’s typical subtropical winter pattern under stable high-pressure systems, with light winds and mostly clear skies allowing modest daytime heating from overnight lows near 10–12°C. Forecast spreads remain modest due to limited synoptic variability this time of year, supporting the heavy weighting on 22°C while leaving modest room for 23°C if insolation increases slightly. Updated official guidance from regional meteorological services over the next 24 hours will likely refine these market-implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service and ensemble model guidance indicate a hot, humid ridge over the Northeast on July 15, with southerly flow and limited cloud cover supporting afternoon highs near 98–100 °F in Central Park. The market’s tight split between the 98–99 °F and 100–101 °F bins reflects modest run-to-run variability in peak boundary-layer temperatures, dew-point depression, and urban heat-island amplification. Historical July maxima average 85 °F, so this pattern represents a strong positive anomaly driven by the same mid-level ridge that produced earlier heat-wave conditions. Traders are weighting the latest deterministic and probabilistic outputs from the GFS, ECMWF, and NBM, which still show a narrow window for either slight under- or over-performance depending on the precise timing of any weak shortwave or wind shift.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the highest temperature in Atlanta on July 15 most likely falling between 86–89°F, with those two buckets nearly tied at roughly one-third each, reflecting modest uncertainty in the latest National Weather Service model runs. Mid-July climatology favors southerly flow under a ridge that typically supports highs in the mid-to-upper 80s, but subtle differences in forecast timing of any weak front, afternoon convection, or boundary-layer mixing could nudge the maximum a degree or two higher or lower. Ensemble spreads of several degrees underscore the normal variability in short-range temperature guidance, with updated model cycles and surface observations tomorrow expected to narrow the range before market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current National Weather Service guidance and model consensus point to a Miami high near 92–94°F on July 15, with afternoon sea-breeze convergence and scattered thunderstorms expected to moderate peak heating after midday. These factors create the narrow spread between the 92–93°F and 94–95°F brackets that together hold nearly 90% of market-implied probability. Diurnal temperature rise is typically capped by the Atlantic breeze arriving between 1–3 p.m. local time and by convective cloud cover that reduces insolation; small shifts in breeze timing or storm initiation can swing the daily maximum by 2–3°F. Historical July climatology at MIA shows a median high of 90°F with a standard deviation of roughly 3°F, underscoring the genuine uncertainty reflected in the closely matched leading outcomes. Updated model runs and the 1 p.m. NWS forecast discussion will be the next key data points for traders.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model runs highlight increased cloud cover and scattered showers tied to a weak Gulf disturbance and subtle frontal boundary, which are limiting daytime solar heating and keeping the Austin high in the upper 70s to low 80s. These conditions contrast with typical mid-July ridging that produces 90–95°F highs, explaining why traders have concentrated nearly two-thirds of implied probability in the 78–81°F bands. Key variables include boundary timing and moisture depth, which could shift the peak by a few degrees; the next NWS update and afternoon observations will provide the clearest resolution signals before markets close.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Persistent cloud cover and scattered thunderstorms are suppressing daytime heating in Houston, driving market-implied odds toward the 82–87°F range for the July 15 high.** Recent model consensus from sources such as the National Weather Service and commercial outlets shows widespread morning to afternoon convection on July 14–15, with 50–70% storm probabilities that limit solar insolation and produce highs several degrees below the 1991–2020 normal of 94°F. 

This pattern follows several days of unsettled conditions tied to elevated moisture and weak steering flow, which have already delivered highs in the low-to-mid 80s on July 13–14. Key differentiating variables among the tightly bunched leading bins (84–85°F at 31%, 86–87°F at 25%, 82–83°F at 21%) include the exact timing and coverage of storms, boundary-layer mixing, and any late-day clearing that could allow brief additional warming. Higher bins above 88°F carry lower probability because sustained sunshine or a stronger ridge would be required, while sub-82°F outcomes remain unlikely absent an unusually robust outflow or marine influence. Updated short-range model runs and radar trends through the afternoon of July 14 remain the primary near-term catalysts for any further shifts in trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Marine layer depth and persistent onshore flow from the Pacific continue to moderate maximum temperatures across Los Angeles on July 15, with National Weather Service guidance and model consensus pointing to highs near 78–82°F at official stations. Recent forecast updates show a slight warming trend under strengthening high pressure, tempered by typical July marine influence that often caps coastal readings below inland peaks. Trader probabilities cluster tightly around 78–83°F because small shifts in boundary layer moisture, wind direction, or afternoon clearing can alter the daily maximum by several degrees. Historical July averages near 83–85°F provide context, while resolution hinges on the official downtown Los Angeles observation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models from the National Weather Service and ensemble runs indicate San Francisco highs on July 15 will likely settle in the 70–73°F range, reflecting the dominant influence of the cool California Current and a moderate marine layer that limits daytime warming through fog, stratus, and onshore flow. These conditions typically cap temperatures near seasonal normals of 67–72°F, with slight upward pressure possible if high pressure weakens the layer and allows more solar heating or reduced wind. Lower outcomes would require stronger onshore advection or deeper fog, while rarer spikes above 74°F hinge on offshore flow or clear-sky subsidence—scenarios models currently assign low probability. Traders weigh these atmospheric dynamics against historical July variability at KSFO, where the marine environment keeps extremes infrequent.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the 25–26°C outcomes as nearly even because Mexico City’s high-elevation plateau (≈2,240 m) and mid-July rainy-season convection create narrow margins between these thresholds. Afternoon thunderstorms, driven by diurnal heating and moisture influx from the Pacific and Gulf, frequently develop by early afternoon and limit peak temperatures through cloud shading and evaporative cooling. Recent model guidance shows modest daytime instability with variable timing of convective initiation, allowing slight differences in insolation or storm onset to shift the daily maximum by 1–2°C. Historical July records cluster in the low-to-mid 20s, with occasional clearer periods permitting brief spikes near 26°C when storms are delayed.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Marcos A. Gelabert Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pa/panama-city/MPMG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see near-even odds between 33°C and 34°C for Panama City’s July 15 maximum because official forecasts, including the Met Office projection of 33°C daytime highs, place the outcome at the upper end of the typical July range of 29–33.7°C. The Intertropical Convergence Zone drives frequent afternoon convection and cloud cover that caps extreme heating, keeping most days near the 31°C climatological average while allowing brief clear periods to push readings 1–2°C higher. Recent model consensus shows limited day-to-day variability, so resolution hinges on exact timing of showers and insolation rather than broader seasonal shifts.
